items.title: UnitedHealth (UNH) Sets Sights on Market Comeback After Brutal Quarter
items.description: UnitedHealth Group’s stock (UNH) has hit a rough patch, weighed down by regulatory pressure on its Medicare Advantage programs and rising medical costs. But stepping back from the current turbulence, there’s reason for cautious optimism. UnitedHealth’s strategic direction and diversified business model leave it well-positioned to benefit from long-term healthcare trends, even as it navigates short-term headwinds. All things considered, I remain cautiously bullish on UNH. Medicare Advantage Probe

items.description: The chipmaker reports earnings after the close tonight. Investors are already bidding the shares higher in hopes of good news.

items.title: UnitedHealth Is Supporting the Dow. It's Not What It Used to Be.
items.description: UnitedHealth Group’s grip on the Dow has weakened, but the health insurance stock still managed to buoy the index on Wednesday.  With UnitedHealth up 2.3%, it was one of only five Dow stocks rising on the day.  With the Dow down 150 points, or 0.4%, UnitedHealth was adding 41.7 points to the index on its own.
